_comment: sub_phase -> {role, intent, required_inputs}. The audit sub-agent is told the role's INTENT and the upstream bindings the call SHOULD carry, so it can judge prompt economy and materialization fidelity against the call's actual purpose. Sourced from the Phase 1-8 role inventory.

product_intent_discovery.intent: Extract product-layer content (personas, journeys, phasing, decisions, requirements, UX) from the raw intent — one of five parallel extraction passes (1.0b).

technical_constraints_discovery.intent: Transcribe every explicitly stated tech decision (frontend, backend, DB, runtime, deployment) from the raw intent as Pre-Approved TECH-* constraints (1.0c).

compliance_retention_discovery.intent: Extract compliance regimes, legal retention obligations and audit requirements as complianceExtractedItems (1.0d).

vv_requirements_discovery.intent: Extract measurable performance/availability/reliability/security targets as V&V requirements with explicit thresholds (1.0e).

canonical_vocabulary_discovery.intent: Extract domain terms and definitions to build the canonical glossary (1.0f).

intent_domain_bloom.intent: Expand the raw intent into multiple candidate product/feature interpretations, each with assumptions, constraints, open questions (1.2).

business_domains_bloom.intent: Propose all business domains and confirm/extend personas for the product (bloom round 1, 1.2).

user_journey_bloom.intent: Propose all user journeys from accepted personas and domains (1.3a).

user_journey_decomposition.intent: Decompose one umbrella journey into atomic child journeys, one level at a time.

system_workflow_bloom.intent: Propose all system workflows backing accepted journeys plus schedule/event/compliance-driven flows (1.3b).

system_workflow_decomposition.intent: Decompose one umbrella workflow into atomic child workflows, one level at a time.

entities_bloom.intent: Propose all persistent data entities required by accepted domains and workflows (bloom round 3, 1.4).

integrations_qa_bloom.intent: Propose external integrations and cross-cutting quality attributes (bloom round 4, 1.5).

product_description_synthesis.intent: Refine only four narrative fields of the product-description handoff (product_vision, product_description, summary, open_loops) after bloom settles (1.6).

release_plan.intent: Propose a release plan — how many releases and which journeys go in which release (1.8).

fr_bloom_skeleton.intent: Generate the FR skeleton — user stories with basic structure but thin ACs (pass 1 of 3, 2.1).

fr_bloom_enrichment.intent: Enrich the FR skeleton with fully articulated acceptance criteria (pass 2 of 3, 2.1).

fr_saturation.intent: Decompose a SINGLE FR/user-story tier by tier (classify-first: atomic_leaf / decomposable / invalid_parent) — 2.1a. Scope is ONE node + its immediate context.

nfr_bloom_skeleton.intent: Generate the NFR skeleton (performance, availability, security, compliance, UX) without thresholds (pass 1 of 3, 2.2).

nfr_bloom_enrichment.intent: Enrich NFRs with measurement thresholds and methods (pass 2 of 3, 2.2).

nfr_saturation.intent: Decompose a SINGLE NFR tier by tier (classify-first) — 2.2a. Scope is ONE node + its immediate context.

system_boundary.intent: Define what is in-scope vs out-of-scope vs external-integration territory (3.1).

system_requirements.intent: Derive formal system requirements (SR-*) from FR+NFR, allocated to the system boundary (3.2).

interface_contracts.intent: Specify interface contracts (protocol, auth, error handling) for every external system and key internal interface (3.3).

software_domains.intent: Identify software domains (bounded contexts) and map each to one or more Phase-1 DOM-* business domains (4.1).

component_skeleton.intent: Decompose the system into components with responsibilities, dependencies and traces_to user stories; distinguish functional vs cross-cutting (4.2).

component_saturation.intent: Tier-based decomposition of a SINGLE component (classify-first: atomic_component / decomposable / invalid_parent) — 4.2a. Scope is ONE component; active_constraints should be narrowed to that component's lineage.

adr_capture.intent: Capture architectural decision records for every significant choice; tag each to the component(s) it governs (4.3).

data_model_skeleton.intent: Specify data-model entities, fields, types and relationships for each component (5.1).

data_model_saturation.intent: Tier-based decomposition of a SINGLE data-model entity (classify-first: atomic_value / decomposable / invalid_parent) — 5.1a. Scope is ONE entity + its component context.

api_definitions.intent: Specify API endpoints (paths, methods, inputs, outputs, auth, error codes) for each component's external interface (5.2).

error_handling.intent: Specify how each component detects, classifies, responds to and surfaces errors (5.3).

configuration_parameters.intent: Specify externally configurable parameters (name, type, default, required) per component (5.4).

task_skeleton.intent: Decompose ONE component's responsibilities into atomic implementation tasks, each binding to specific leaf AC-* ids THIS component implements (6.1). The AC menu is a reference lookup — the task should cite only the component's own leaf ACs, not the whole catalog.

task_reconciliation.intent: Generate tasks to cover leaf ACs missed by the per-component skeleton passes; receives ONLY the uncovered AC ids + a compact component routing menu (6.1 reconciliation).

task_saturation.intent: Tier-based decomposition of a SINGLE implementation task (classify-first: atomic_unit / decomposable / invalid_parent) — 6.1a. Scope is ONE task + its component context; active_constraints should be narrowed to the task's inherited TECH-* ids.

test_case_skeleton.intent: Generate structured test-case specs for every acceptance criterion (functional, integration, property-based), grouped into per-component suites (7.1).

test_case_saturation.intent: Tier-based decomposition of a SINGLE test case (classify-first: atomic_step / decomposable / invalid_parent) — 7.1a. Scope is ONE test case + the ACs IT validates; the AC summary should be scoped to the parent's own ACs, not the full catalog.

evaluation_design.intent: Design functional, quality (NFR) and reasoning evaluation criteria NOT already covered by the test plan (8.1).
